{{Otheruses||山梨県甲府市にある古墳（おてんぐさん古墳/天狗山古墳）|天狗山 (甲府市)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天狗山古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]真備町下二万（字矢形）・川辺（字南山） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 32.12 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 10.10 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長60m
高さ9m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]（内部に組合式木棺） |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半-末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 倉敷市指定史跡「天狗山古墳付天狗山西古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-西 |ラベル = 天狗山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''天狗山古墳'''（てんぐやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]下二万（しもにま）・川辺（かわべ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。倉敷市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!埋葬施設!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |'''天狗山古墳'''||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長60m||[[竪穴式石室]]||5c後半-末||市史跡 |- |[[二万大塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長38m||[[横穴式石室]]||6c中||市史跡 |- |[[箭田大塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径46m||横穴式石室||6c後半||国史跡 |}
岡山県南部の[[小田川 (高梁川水系)|小田川]]・[[高梁川]]の合流点付近、南山山塊の一支丘上（標高約90メートル）に築造された古墳である。南西には天狗山西古墳（天狗山2号墳）が所在する。戦前に乱掘されて副葬品が出土しているほか、[[1997年|1997]]-[[1999年]]度（[[平成]]9-11年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められるほか、[[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・蓋形埴輪）が認められる{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。また墳丘周囲には盾形の周溝・周堤が巡らされる{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。埋葬施設は後円部下の深部における[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に組合式木棺を据える。石室内からは銅鏡・武具など多数の副葬品が出土しており、特に[[小札甲]]（挂甲）・籠手は当時の甲冑の実態を知るうえで注目される資料になる。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半-末頃と推定される{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。[[造山古墳]]・[[作山古墳]]の勢力の衰退後に築造されるほか、[[二万大塚古墳]]（倉敷市真備町下二万）・[[箭田大塚古墳]]（倉敷市真備町箭田）へと続く[[下道氏]]の台頭の契機と考えられるなど、吉備地方における政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[2005年]]（平成17年）に倉敷市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * 戦前、天狗山古墳の乱掘。副葬品の出土（現在は[[東京国立博物館]]所蔵）。 * [[1997年|1997]]-[[1999年]]度（[[平成]]9-11年度）、天狗山古墳・天狗山西古墳の発掘調査（[[岡山大学]]考古学研究室、[[2014年]]に報告）。 * [[2005年]]（平成17年）12月5日、「天狗山古墳付天狗山西古墳」として倉敷市指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。 * 古墳総長：85メートル - 周溝・周堤を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：60メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：46メートル ** 高さ：9メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：17メートル ** 幅：20メートル ** 高さ：2メートル 墳丘周囲には盾形の周溝・周堤が巡らされており、周堤は幅9メートル・高さ2メートルを測る{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。埴輪は、墳頂部・テラス部・周堤で認められる{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては後円部において[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。墳頂下5メートルという深部に位置し、盛土前の構築とされる{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。石室は長さ4メートル・幅1メートル・幅0.8メートルを測る。角礫を積み粘土を間に詰めることによって構築されており、床面には玉砂利を敷き詰め、壁面には赤色顔料を塗布する史跡説明板。。 石室内には組合式木棺を据えており、木棺に使用した鎹が認められる{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。石室内からは多数の副葬品が出土している。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:天狗山古墳出土 挂甲 (J-37088-1).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|小札甲（挂甲）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:天狗山古墳出土 籠手 (J-37088-2).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|籠手}}{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}}]] [[ファイル:天狗山古墳出土 剣菱形杏葉 (J-37096).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|剣菱形杏葉}}{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}}]] 石室内から出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}。 * 変形獣形鏡 * 装身具 ** 勾玉 ** 櫛 * 武器・武具 ** 大刀 ** 剣 ** 鏃 ** [[小札甲]]（挂甲） - 畳み込まれた状態で銹着しており、小札甲の実態を知るうえで有用な資料とされる{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。 ** 筒籠手 ** 胡簶 * 馬具 ** 轡 ** 杏葉 ** 雲珠 ** 鞖 ** 鐙 * 農工具 ** 鋤先 ** 刀子 == 文化財 == === 倉敷市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 天狗山古墳付天狗山西古墳 - 2005年（平成17年）12月5日指定[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm 天狗山古墳付天狗山西古墳]（倉敷市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * [[岡山大学]]考古資料展示室（岡山市北区津島中） - 岡山大学キャンパス内。天狗山古墳の出土品を保管。 * [[東京国立博物館]]（東京都台東区） - 天狗山古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（倉敷市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]34|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|岡山県の地名|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大塚初重|authorlink=大塚初重|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=亀山行雄|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=村井嵒雄|title=岡山県天狗山古墳出土の遺物|date=1972|journal=Museum|publisher=東京国立博物館|volume=|number=250|pages=4-17|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://ousar.lib.okayama-u.ac.jp/files/public/6/63475/20220511140130181679/fulltext20220511-1_1.pdf 天狗山古墳]}}|publisher=岡山大学考古学研究室・天狗山古墳発掘調査団|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは岡山大学学術成果リポジトリ。 == 関連項目 == * [[二万大塚古墳]] * [[箭田大塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm 天狗山古墳付天狗山西古墳] - 倉敷市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:てんくやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:倉敷市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:岡山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[天狗山古墳]] to [[Tengu-yama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

岡山大学キャンパス内。天狗山古墳の出土品を保管。 * [[東京国立博物館]]（東京都台東区） - 天狗山古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（倉敷市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]34|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|岡山県の地名|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大塚初重|authorlink=大塚初重|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=亀山行雄|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|続日本古墳大辞典|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=村井嵒雄|title=岡山県天狗山古墳出土の遺物|date=1972|journal=Museum|publisher=東京国立博物館|volume=|number=250|pages=4-17|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://ousar.lib.okayama-u.ac.jp/files/public/6/63475/20220511140130181679/fulltext20220511-1_1.pdf 天狗山古墳]}}|publisher=岡山大学考古学研究室・天狗山古墳発掘調査団|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは岡山大学学術成果リポジトリ。 == 関連項目 == * [[二万大塚古墳]] * [[箭田大塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm 天狗山古墳付天狗山西古墳] - 倉敷市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:てんくやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:倉敷市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:岡山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

'''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in {{ill|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|箭田大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun] – Kurashiki City official website

Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.

It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.

<!--Tengu-yama Kofun--><!--Tengu-yama Kofun--><!--Tengu-yama Kofun--><!--Tengu-yama Kofun--> [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tenguyama Kofun}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun] – Kurashiki City official website

== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun] – Kurashiki City official website

Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun] – Kurashiki City official website
{{Kofun navbar}}

== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.

<!--Tengu-yama Kofun--><!--Tengu-yama Kofun--><!--Tengu-yama Kofun--><!--Tengu-yama Kofun--> [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tenguyama Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q114481278}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun] – Kurashiki City official website

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q114481278]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun] – Kurashiki City official website

Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.

{{wikidata link|Q114481278}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture}}, Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11603302|lt=Yata Ōtsuka Kofun}}

{{wikidata link|Q114481278}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tenguyama Kofun]][[ja:天狗山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.625589|N|133.719472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}}}} '''Tengu-yama Kofun''' (天狗山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Kurashiki}}, Japan. It is classified as a scallop-shaped kofun (帆立貝形古墳) and has been designated as a municipal historic site by the city of Kurashiki.
== Overview == The Tengu-yama Kofun is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 90 meters, near the confluence of the Odagawa and Takahashi Rivers in southern Okayama Prefecture. Adjacent to it is the Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (Tengu-yama 2nd Mound). The site was subject to looting before World War II, and subsequent excavations were conducted from 1997 to 1999.
The mound features a short, scallop-shaped keyhole structure, with the front section oriented westward. It is a two-tiered mound with traces of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa (clay figures), including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and lid-shaped haniwa. A shield-shaped moat and embankment surround the mound. The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室) located deep within the rear circular portion, containing a composite wooden coffin. Artifacts, such as bronze mirrors and armor, have been recovered, offering valuable insights into kofun-period armor and burial practices.
The construction of Tengu-yama Kofun is dated to the late 5th century, corresponding to the late Middle Kofun Period. It is believed to mark the decline of the Zōzan and Sakuyama kofun builders and the emergence of the Shimotsumichi clan in the Kibi region.
== Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total mound length: 85 meters (including the moat and embankment) * Mound length: 60 meters * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 46 meters ** Height: 9 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 17 meters ** Width: 20 meters ** Height: 2 meters
The embankment surrounding the mound measures 9 meters in width and 2 meters in height. Haniwa have been discovered on the mound's summit, terraces, and embankments.
== Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a vertical stone structure located 5 meters beneath the mound’s summit. It measures 4 meters in length, 1 meter in width, and 0.8 meters in height. The walls are constructed from angular stones packed with clay and coated with red pigment. A composite wooden coffin was placed within the chamber, evidenced by surviving iron staples.
Artifacts recovered from the chamber include: * A deformed animal-shaped mirror * Ornaments: ** Magatama beads ** Combs * Weapons and armor: ** Swords ** Small and large blades ** Iron arrowheads ** Small-scale armor plates (挂甲) – folded and corroded, providing rare data on ancient armor. ** Tube-shaped gauntlets ** Quivers * Horse trappings: ** Bridle bits ** Decorative fittings ** Bells ** Saddles ** Stirrups * Agricultural tools: ** Hoe blades ** Small knives
== Cultural Significance == The artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun, particularly the armor and weapons, contribute significantly to understanding the political and military dynamics of the late Kofun Period in the Kibi region. The site's construction marked a shift in power from earlier kofun builders to the Shimotsumichi clan.
== Cultural Property Designation == === Kurashiki Municipal Designation === * Historic Site: Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun (designated December 5, 2005)
== Associated Facilities == * {{ill|Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja|岡山大学考古資料展示室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama University Archaeological Research Room|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Holds artifacts from Tengu-yama Kofun. * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} – Preserves additional artifacts.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系34|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Hajime Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yukio Kameyama|year=2002|chapter=天狗山古墳|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|二万大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niman Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11603302|lt=Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Yata Ōtsuka Kofun}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5532.htm Tengu-yama Kofun and Tengu-yama Nishi Kofun] – Kurashiki City official website
